proposed_hypotheses.text: There exist features in the dataset that show statistically significant univariate association with pfs_months.

analyses.code: Univariate screen: t-test for binary features, Spearman rho for ordinal/continuous, ANOVA for categorical. Outcome=pfs_months across all 124 features.
analyses.result_summary: Strongest univariate signals (smallest p, all p<<1e-50): feature_078 (Spearman r=+0.785), feature_057 (rho=-0.36), feature_051 (mean diff -1.35 mo), feature_038 (+0.97 mo), feature_099 (rho=-0.12), feature_092 (rho=+0.10), feature_013 (-0.33), feature_043 (-0.31), feature_009 (rho=-0.05), feature_109 (-0.23), feature_067 (+0.22).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_078 (continuous, range 30-90, likely age) is associated with longer pfs_months in this CRC cohort.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_078; quartile means of pfs_months by feature_078 quartile.
analyses.result_summary: feature_078 is strongly positively associated with pfs_months. Univariate slope = +0.176 mo per unit (p < 1e-300). Quartile means: Q1 (30-58.3) 2.09 mo; Q2 (58.3-65) 3.71 mo; Q3 (65-71.8) 4.90 mo; Q4 (71.8-90) 6.59 mo. Direction confirmed; effect persists in adjusted models (beta = +0.176, p ~0).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_057 (ordinal 0/1/2) is associated with shorter pfs_months in a dose-response fashion (each step worse PFS by approximately 1 month).

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ C(feature_057) + adjusters (feature_078, feature_051, feature_038, feature_092). Stratified means.
analyses.result_summary: feature_057 shows a clean dose-response: feature_057=0 mean 5.27 mo (n=17,592), =1 mean 4.05 mo (n=24,971), =2 mean 2.91 mo (n=7,437). Adjusted coefficients vs reference 0: level 1 beta=-1.198 (p<1e-300), level 2 beta=-2.337 (p<1e-300). Strongly supports staged/grade-like prognostic.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_051=1 (binary, ~55% prevalence) have shorter pfs_months than those with feature_051=0.

analyses.code: Welch t-test feature_051=1 vs 0; OLS adjusted for stage, age, treatment.
analyses.result_summary: feature_051=1 (n=27,481) mean pfs 3.70 mo vs feature_051=0 (n=22,519) mean 5.05 mo. Mean difference = -1.352 mo (p<1e-300). Adjusted for age, stage, feature_038, feature_092: beta=-1.356 (p<1e-300). Effect is robust across stage strata (-1.40 in stage 0, -1.35 in stage 1, -1.27 in stage 2).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_038=1 (binary, ~20% prevalence) have longer pfs_months than those with feature_038=0, suggesting feature_038 is a beneficial therapy.

analyses.code: Welch t-test; OLS pfs_months ~ feature_038 + adjusters.
analyses.result_summary: feature_038=1 mean pfs 5.09 mo (n=10,022) vs feature_038=0 mean 4.12 mo (n=39,978). Crude difference +0.97 mo (p ~ 1.7e-216). Adjusted (full model): beta = +0.943 mo (p<1e-300). Direction and significance confirmed. (Note: this is the crude main effect; iteration 11-13 reveals strong effect modification.)

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_092 (continuous, 1.5-5.5, plausibly serum albumin) is associated with longer pfs_months independent of age and stage.
Higher feature_099 (continuous, 0-24) is associated with shorter pfs_months independent of age and stage.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_092 + feature_078 + C(feature_057) + feature_051 + feature_038.
analyses.result_summary: feature_092 adjusted beta = +0.470 mo per unit (p < 1e-300). Direction positive: every 1-unit higher associates with about a half-month longer PFS, consistent with a favorable prognostic biomarker.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_099 + adjusters; binned means of pfs_months across feature_099 ranges.
analyses.result_summary: feature_099 adjusted beta = -0.077 mo per unit (p < 1e-300). Binned means: 0 -> 4.59, (0,5] -> 4.39, (5,10] -> 4.10, (10,25] -> 3.63. Monotonic, dose-dependent decrement. Direction negative confirmed.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_009 (continuous, 0-777) has a small negative association with pfs_months independent of age, stage, and known treatments.
feature_006 (continuous, 48-830) has a small negative association with pfs_months after adjustment.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_009 + adjusters.
analyses.result_summary: feature_009 adjusted beta = -0.0050 mo per unit (p ~ 2.7e-124). Small per-unit, but very precisely estimated and direction negative. Supported.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_006 + adjusters.
analyses.result_summary: feature_006 adjusted beta = -0.0005 mo per unit (p ~ 1.4e-33). Direction negative; small effect per unit but precisely estimated.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across feature_064 (race: white/black/hispanic/asian/other) categories overall.
Mean pfs_months differs across feature_018 (insurance: medicare/private/medicaid/uninsured) categories overall.

analyses.code: ANOVA across feature_064; group means.
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months by race: white 4.32 (n=32,456), hispanic 4.31, asian 4.31, black 4.27, other 4.41. Range 0.13 mo. ANOVA p=0.219 -- no significant overall difference.

analyses.code: ANOVA across feature_018; group means.
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months by insurance: private 4.33, medicare 4.31, medicaid 4.28, uninsured 4.25. Range 0.08 mo. Differences are small and not clinically meaningful (overall p > 0.05).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Receipt of feature_038 (the apparently beneficial therapy) differs by race (feature_064).
Receipt of feature_038 differs by insurance (feature_018).

analyses.code: Crosstab feature_064 x feature_038 receipt rates; chi-square.
analyses.result_summary: feature_038 receipt: asian 0.211, black 0.201, hispanic 0.198, other 0.192, white 0.200. Range 0.019 (~2 percentage points), chi-square p > 0.1. No meaningful disparity.

analyses.code: Crosstab feature_018 x feature_038 receipt rates.
analyses.result_summary: feature_038 receipt: medicaid 0.198, medicare 0.202, private 0.201, uninsured 0.190. Range 0.012; differences not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The beneficial effect of feature_038 on pfs_months is reduced (smaller positive effect) in patients with feature_013=1 vs feature_013=0.
The beneficial effect of feature_038 on pfs_months is reduced in patients with feature_043=1 vs feature_043=0.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_038 * feature_013 + adjusters.
analyses.result_summary: Interaction term feature_038:feature_013 beta = -1.78 mo (p < 1e-300). In feature_013=0 patients, feature_038 confers approximately +2.3 mo PFS; in feature_013=1, the marginal benefit of feature_038 is essentially zero. Direction (effect-modifying) confirmed.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_038 * feature_043 + adjusters.
analyses.result_summary: Interaction term feature_038:feature_043 beta = -1.47 mo (p < 1e-300). feature_043=1 erodes feature_038 benefit by about -1.47 mo. Direction confirmed; feature_043 acts as a resistance/non-response marker for feature_038.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_109 (binary, ~5% prev) reduces the benefit of feature_038 on pfs_months when feature_109=1.
feature_067 (binary, ~3% prev) increases the benefit of feature_038 on pfs_months when feature_067=1 (sensitivity marker).

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_038 * feature_109 + adjusters.
analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = -1.77 mo (p ~ 1.5e-102). In feature_109=0 cohort feature_038 yields +1.0 mo crude; in feature_109=1 cohort yields -0.16 mo. Confirms feature_109 as a feature_038-resistance modifier.

analyses.code: Stratified means feature_038 effect by feature_067 in biomarker-negative subset; OLS interaction.
analyses.result_summary: In the unadjusted interaction, beta = +0.89 mo (p ~ 1.8e-56). However, in the biomarker-negative subgroup (where feature_038 is broadly active) the additional gain from feature_067=1 is modest (+2.91 vs +2.75 mo). After adjusting for feature_013/043/109 in a joint model, the feature_038:feature_067 interaction shrinks to +0.07 mo (p=0.15). feature_067 appears partially confounded with the resistance markers and is not an independent sensitivity marker.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with all three resistance markers negative (feature_013=0, feature_043=0, feature_109=0) experience a large positive effect of feature_038 on pfs_months (>2 months mean difference).
Patients with any of feature_013=1, feature_043=1, or feature_109=1 experience no clinically meaningful effect of feature_038 on pfs_months.

analyses.code: Subset to (feature_013==0)&(feature_043==0)&(feature_109==0); Welch t-test feature_038=1 vs 0.
analyses.result_summary: Biomarker-triple-negative subgroup n=17,372. feature_038=1 mean pfs 6.88 (n=3,461) vs feature_038=0 mean 4.12 (n=13,911). Mean diff = +2.76 mo (p < 1e-300). Strongly supported.

analyses.code: Subset to feature_013|feature_043|feature_109==1; t-test.
analyses.result_summary: Resistance-marker positive subgroup n=32,628. feature_038=1 mean pfs 4.16 vs =0 mean 4.12. Mean diff = +0.04 mo, p=0.16. No clinically or statistically meaningful benefit. Supported: feature_038 benefit is restricted to biomarker-negative patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013 status alone (in the absence of feature_038) does not affect baseline pfs_months: it is a purely predictive (not prognostic) biomarker.
feature_043 status alone (in the absence of feature_038) does not affect baseline pfs_months: it is a purely predictive (not prognostic) biomarker.

analyses.code: Among feature_038=0 only: cross-tab pfs_months means by (feature_013, feature_043).
analyses.result_summary: Among feature_038=0 patients (n=39,978): mean PFS by (f013,f043) -- (0,0):4.12, (0,1):4.11, (1,0):4.12, (1,1):4.10. Differences <0.03 mo, all p>0.5. Supports both hypotheses: feature_013 and feature_043 are NOT prognostic without feature_038, only predictive of feature_038 benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In the biomarker-negative subgroup, the magnitude of feature_038 benefit on pfs_months is similar across racial groups (feature_064).

analyses.code: OLS within biomarker-negative subgroup: pfs_months ~ feature_038 * C(feature_064) + adjusters.
analyses.result_summary: Reference group benefit (asian) = +2.75 mo. Interaction coefficients vs reference: black +0.030 (p=0.78), hispanic +0.093 (p=0.36), other +0.302 (p=0.08), white -0.056 (p=0.53). Stratified mean differences: white +2.63, black +2.79, hispanic +3.21, asian +2.88, other +3.07. No significant race-based heterogeneity. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In the biomarker-negative subgroup, feature_038 benefit is similar across insurance categories (feature_018), with possibly modestly smaller benefit in uninsured patients.

analyses.code: OLS within biomarker-negative subgroup: pfs_months ~ feature_038 * C(feature_018) + adjusters.
analyses.result_summary: Reference (medicaid) feature_038 effect = +2.81 mo. Interaction vs reference: medicare -0.061 (p=0.35), private -0.094 (p=0.16), uninsured -0.377 (p=0.0021). Stratified diffs: medicaid +3.09, medicare +2.80, private +2.60, uninsured +2.75. The uninsured interaction is modest in magnitude (~0.4 mo) and statistically significant; race shows no such pattern. Partially supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_051 on pfs_months is similar across racial groups.
The negative effect of feature_051 on pfs_months is similar across insurance groups.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_051 * C(feature_064) + adjusters.
analyses.result_summary: Reference group feature_051 effect = -1.345 mo. Interaction terms: black -0.007 (p=0.88), hispanic -0.024 (p=0.57), other +0.046 (p=0.51), white -0.014 (p=0.70). All p > 0.05 -- no race heterogeneity. Supported.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_051 * C(feature_018) + adjusters.
analyses.result_summary: Reference effect = -1.397 mo. Interactions: medicare +0.049 (p=0.06), private +0.050 (p=0.06), uninsured -0.022 (p=0.65). Marginal but not significant. Supported (no clear insurance heterogeneity).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_051 on pfs_months grows larger (more negative) with increasing feature_078 (age).

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_051 * feature_078 + adjusters.
analyses.result_summary: feature_051:feature_078 interaction beta = -0.0067 mo per year (p ~ 9.9e-18). Stratified diffs by age tertile: youngest -1.28, middle -1.36, oldest -1.40. The harmful effect of feature_051 is slightly more pronounced in older patients, but the magnitude (~0.12 mo across the age range) is small in absolute terms. Direction confirmed.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_038 and feature_051 effects on pfs_months are approximately additive (no strong interaction between the two).

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_051 * feature_038 + adjusters.
analyses.result_summary: feature_051:feature_038 interaction beta = +0.034 mo (p = 0.081). 4-cell means: f51=0,f38=0 -> 4.87; f51=0,f38=1 -> 5.81; f51=1,f38=0 -> 3.50; f51=1,f38=1 -> 4.51. The benefit of feature_038 is roughly the same in feature_051=0 (+0.94) as in feature_051=1 (+1.01). Effects are additive. Supported (interaction NS).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_071 (ordinal 0-10) has no monotone or significant association with pfs_months.

analyses.code: Spearman rho; ordered group means; OLS adjusted slope.
analyses.result_summary: Means by feature_071 level (0..10): 4.32, 4.32, 4.30, 4.27, 4.30, 4.40, 4.29, 4.28, 4.36, 4.29, 4.19. No monotone trend. Adjusted slope = -0.0007 mo per unit, p = 0.65. Hypothesis supported -- feature_071 is unrelated to outcome.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the remaining ~75 binary features (excluding the established treatment/biomarker set) at least one substantially modifies the effect of feature_038 on pfs_months (interaction effect > 0.2 mo and p < 0.001).

analyses.code: Pairwise OLS pfs_months ~ feature_038 * binary_feat + adjusters; ranked by p.
analyses.result_summary: Top hits all have small interaction magnitudes: feature_086 (-0.110, p=1.3e-6), feature_030 (+0.120, p=6.2e-6), feature_052 (+0.128, p=9.4e-6), feature_041 (-0.136, p=1.6e-5), feature_040 (-0.214, p=4.6e-5), feature_001 (+0.214, p=3.6e-4). Several of these reach p<0.001 but the largest interaction magnitude (~0.21 mo) is much smaller than the 1.5-1.8 mo modifications driven by feature_013/043/109. Hypothesis weakly supported (statistically), but the modifiers found are minor relative to the main biomarker pattern.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of feature_038 on pfs_months in the biomarker-negative subgroup does not depend on baseline feature_092 (likely albumin) tertile.

analyses.code: Stratified means within biomarker-negative cohort by feature_092 tertile.
analyses.result_summary: Mean diff (feature_038=1 minus =0) across feature_092 tertiles in biomarker-negative subset: low +2.84, mid +2.74, high +2.69. Range 0.15 mo across tertiles. Largely consistent benefit. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_109 is not a strong baseline (prognostic) factor for pfs_months when not receiving feature_038.

analyses.code: Means by feature_109 in feature_038=0 subset; t-test.
analyses.result_summary: Among feature_038=0: feature_109=0 mean 4.13, feature_109=1 mean 4.30. Difference of -0.17 mo is small (and slightly *positive* for feature_109=1, opposite of crude univariate). Supported -- like feature_013/043, feature_109 acts mainly as a feature_038-predictive marker, not as a prognostic factor on its own.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among biomarker-positive patients, feature_038 is administered at the same rate as among biomarker-negative patients across racial groups (no targeted-therapy mis-allocation by race).
Same hypothesis across insurance groups.

analyses.code: Stratified mean of feature_038 by race within biomarker-positive (any of 013/043/109==1) subgroup.
analyses.result_summary: feature_038 receipt in biomarker-positive subgroup: asian 0.210, black 0.200, hispanic 0.200, other 0.196, white 0.201. ~20% in every race. Suggests feature_038 is given without regard to biomarker status (potential overuse), but the rate is uniform across racial groups -- no race-based mis-allocation.

analyses.code: Stratified mean of feature_038 by insurance within biomarker-positive subgroup.
analyses.result_summary: feature_038 receipt in biomarker-positive subgroup by insurance: medicaid 0.197, medicare 0.205, private 0.200, uninsured 0.189. Uniform allocation. Supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariable model that includes feature_078, feature_057, feature_051, feature_092, feature_099, feature_009, feature_006, plus feature_038 with interactions on feature_013/043/109, explains the majority (>80%) of variance in pfs_months.

analyses.code: OLS with feature_078 + C(feature_057) + feature_051 + feature_038*feature_013 + feature_038*feature_043 + feature_038*feature_109 + feature_092 + feature_099 + feature_009 + feature_006 + C(feature_064) + C(feature_018).
analyses.result_summary: N=50,000, Adj R^2 = 0.898. Significant terms (all p<0.001): feature_078 +0.176/yr, feature_057=1 -1.192, feature_057=2 -2.319, feature_051 -1.356, feature_038 +2.289, feature_038:feature_013 -1.784, feature_038:feature_043 -1.474, feature_038:feature_109 -1.769, feature_092 +0.472/unit, feature_099 -0.077/unit, feature_009 -0.005/unit, feature_006 -0.0005/unit. Race/insurance terms not significant after adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After multivariable adjustment, race (feature_064) and insurance (feature_018) contribute negligibly to pfs_months, both as main effects and as effect modifiers of feature_038/feature_051.

analyses.code: Joint Wald-style examination of all race/insurance main and interaction terms across the iteration-14, -15, -16 models and the iteration-24 multivariable.
analyses.result_summary: All race main effects: |coef| < 0.10 mo, p > 0.4. All insurance main effects: |coef| < 0.05 mo, p > 0.3. All race x feature_038 interactions: |coef| < 0.30, p > 0.08. All race x feature_051 interactions: |coef| < 0.05, p > 0.5. The single statistically significant demographic interaction is feature_038 x uninsured (-0.38 mo, p=0.0021), which is small relative to the main +2.81 mo benefit (~13% relative reduction). Hypothesis supported: this dataset shows no meaningful demographic disparity in either outcome levels or treatment effect.
